children.heading: Private business use test
children.label: 1 Private business use test
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, an issue meets the test of this paragraph if more than 10 percent of the proceeds of the issue are to be used for any private business use.

children.label: i
children.text: property used or to be used for a private business use, or

children.label: ii
children.text: payments in respect of such property, or

children.label: B
children.text: to be derived from payments (whether or not to the issuer) in respect of property, or borrowed money, used or to be used for a private business use.

children.label: III
children.text: payments, property, and borrowed money with respect to any use of proceeds described in subclause (I) or (II).

children.label: B Disproportionate related business use proceeds
i

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “private business use” means use (directly or indirectly) in a trade or business carried on by any person other than a governmental unit. For purposes of the preceding sentence, use as a member of the general public shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Clarification of trade or business
children.label: B Clarification of trade or business
children.text: For purposes of the 1st sentence of subparagraph (A), any activity carried on by a person other than a natural person shall be treated as a trade or business.

children.heading: Clarification relating to qualified carbon dioxide capture facilities
children.label: C Clarification relating to qualified carbon dioxide capture facilities
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the sale of carbon dioxide produced by a qualified carbon dioxide capture facility (as defined in section 142()) which is owned by a governmental unit shall not constitute private business use.o

children.heading: Government use
children.label: 7 Government use
children.text: The term “government use” means any use other than a private business use.

children.label: A
children.text: the proceeds of such issue which are to be used for any private business use, or

children.label: B
children.text: the proceeds of such issue with respect to which there are payments (or property or borrowed money) described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Exception for qualified 501(c)(3) bonds
children.label: 9 Exception for qualified 501(c)(3) bonds
children.text: There shall not be taken into account under this subsection or subsection (c) the portion of the proceeds of an issue which (if issued as a separate issue) would be treated as a qualified 501(c)(3) bond if the issuer elects to treat such portion as a qualified 501(c)(3) bond.

children.heading: Nongovernmental output property
children.label: 2 Nongovernmental output property
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, for purposes of paragraph (1), the term “nongovernmental output property” means any property (or interest therein) which before such acquisition was used (or held for use) by a person other than a governmental unit in connection with an output facility (within the meaning of subsection (b)(4)) (other than a facility for the furnishing of water). For purposes of the preceding sentence, use (or the holding for use) before , shall not be taken into account.October 14, 1987

children.heading: Exception for property acquired to provide output to certain areas
children.label: 3 Exception for property acquired to provide output to certain areas
i
children.text: a qualified service area of the governmental unit acquiring the property, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a qualified annexed area of such unit.

children.heading: Qualified service area
children.label: i Qualified service area
children.text: The term “qualified service area” means, with respect to the governmental unit acquiring the property, any area throughout which such unit provided (at all times during the 10-year period ending on the date such property is acquired by such unit) output of the same type as the output to be provided by such property. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the period before , shall not be taken into account.October 14, 1987

children.label: I
children.text: such area is contiguous to, and annexed for general governmental purposes into, a qualified service area of such unit,

children.label: II
children.text: output from such property is made available to all members of the general public in the annexed area, and

children.label: III
children.text: the annexed area is not greater than 10 percent of such qualified service area.

children.heading: Limitation on size of annexed area not to apply where output capacity does not increase by more than 10 percent
children.label: C Limitation on size of annexed area not to apply where output capacity does not increase by more than 10 percent
children.text: Subclause (III) of subparagraph (B)(ii) shall not apply to an annexation of an area by a governmental unit if the output capacity of the property acquired in connection with the annexation, when added to the output capacity of all other property which is not treated as nongovernmental output property by reason of subparagraph (A)(ii) with respect to such annexed area, does not exceed 10 percent of the output capacity of the property providing output of the same type to the qualified service area into which it is annexed.

children.heading: Rules for determining relative size, etc.
children.label: D Rules for determining relative size, etc.
i
children.text: The size of any qualified service area and the output capacity of property serving such area shall be determined as the close of the calendar year preceding the calendar year in which the acquisition of nongovernmental output property or the annexation occurs.

children.label: ii
children.text: A qualified annexed area shall be treated as part of the qualified service area into which it is annexed for purposes of determining whether any other area annexed in a later year is a qualified annexed area.

children.heading: Exception for property converted to nonoutput use
children.label: 4 Exception for property converted to nonoutput use

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “nongovernmental output property” shall not include any property which is to be converted to a use not in connection with an output facility.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any property which is part of the output function of a nuclear power facility.

children.label: A
children.text: subsections (c) and (d) of section 147 (relating to limitations on acquisition of land and existing property) shall not apply, and

children.label: B
children.text: paragraph (8) of section 142(a) shall be applied as if it did not contain “local”.

children.heading: Treatment of joint action agencies
children.label: 6 Treatment of joint action agencies
children.text: With respect to nongovernmental output property acquired by a joint action agency the members of which are governmental units, this subsection shall be applied at the member level by treating each member as acquiring its proportionate share of such property.

children.heading: Exception for qualified electric and natural gas supply contracts
children.label: 7 Exception for qualified electric and natural gas supply contracts
children.text: The term “nongovernmental output property” shall not include any contract for the prepayment of electricity or natural gas which is not investment property under section 148(b)(2).
